spec_match.rationale: Correctly presents the case-sensitivity differences of CSS Anchor Positioning dashed-ident names (--myAnchor vs --MYANCHOR) under standards and quirks modes, highlighting how Chrome 150 aligns them.

interactive.rationale: Allows typing custom anchor names and positioned tooltip anchor references and immediately calculates whether they resolve or mismatch under Standard, Quirks (pre-150), and Quirks (150+) rules, with live visual connected/disconnected simulation block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the visual CSS anchor-name specifications and the Chrome 150 quirks mode case-sensitivity fix perfectly.

edge_cases.rationale: Correctly handles feature detection of CSS Anchor Positioning using CSS.supports/computedStyles, falling back gracefully to simulated visual updates if the browser lacks support.

references.rationale: Includes correct links back to HTML fragment sibling testers and contains the specific ChromeStatus entry for CSS Anchor positioning (5094803769835520).

design_system.rationale: Beautiful monospace code layout, active toggle states, and contrast-safe emerald/rose outcome states.

openQuestions.title: Support actual rendering test in browser supporting anchors
openQuestions.detail: While it has feature detection for CSS Anchor Positioning, the live toggle box only uses a simulated class switch. If anchor positioning is supported natively, it could apply the actual CSS styles to the DOM nodes to let the browser perform the true case-sensitive anchor layout lookup natively.

summary: A well-constructed explorer for CSS Anchor Positioning dashed-ident name case sensitivity, featuring solid interactive input fields and simulated before/after state lookups.
